name: Carlton
description: The Carlton Football Club, nicknamed the Blues, is based in Melbourne and is one of the oldest and most successful clubs in the AFL, having won 16 VFL/AFL premierships.

name: Richmond
description: The Richmond Football Club, nicknamed the Tigers, is a powerhouse club based at Punt Road Oval. They have a massive supporter base and multiple recent premierships.

name: Collingwood
description: The Collingwood Football Club, the Magpies, is traditionally one of the biggest and most deeply supported (and rivalled) clubs in the AFL, renowned for their black and white stripes.

name: Geelong Cats
description: Based in the regional city of Geelong, the Cats have a long history of sustained success and home dominance at GMHBA Stadium.

name: Melbourne
description: The Melbourne Football Club, known as the Demons, is the world's oldest professional club of any football code, playing their home games at the iconic MCG.

name: Port Adelaide
description: The Port Adelaide Football Club, the Power, has a rich heritage rooted in the SANFL before joining the AFL in 1997, boasting fierce hometown pride.

name: Adelaide Crows
description: The Adelaide Football Club, or Crows, was formed in 1990 as the first South Australian team in the AFL, winning back-to-back premierships in the late 90s.

name: Hawthorn
description: The Hawthorn Football Club, the Hawks, dominated several eras of modern football, famous for their brown and gold stripes and "family club" ethos.

name: Western Bulldogs
description: The Western Bulldogs (formerly Footscray) hail from Melbourne's working-class western suburbs and famously broke a 62-year premiership drought in 2016.

name: Sydney Swans
description: Originally from South Melbourne, the Swans relocated to Sydney in the 1980s and established themselves as a remarkably consistent and deeply respected club.

name: Brisbane Lions
description: Formed from a merger between the Brisbane Bears and Fitzroy Lions, this club achieved a historic three-peat of premierships in the early 2000s.

name: West Coast Eagles
description: Based in Perth, the West Coast Eagles were a pioneering force for the AFL's national expansion, commanding huge crowds and winning multiple flags.

name: St Kilda
description: The St Kilda Football Club, the Saints, is defined by its passionate bayside fan base and iconic red, white, and black tri-colors.

name: Gold Coast SUNS
description: The Gold Coast SUNS are one of the AFL's newer franchises, bringing top-flight football to the sunny tourist strip of Queensland.

name: GWS GIANTS
description: The Greater Western Sydney GIANTS represent Western Sydney and the ACT. Despite being a modern expansion team, they quickly built a formidable and exciting brand.

name: Essendon
description: The Essendon Football Club, the Bombers, is one of the AFL's grand old clubs, sharing the record for the most VFL/AFL premierships (16).

name: Fremantle
description: The Fremantle Dockers joined the AFL in 1995, representing the proud port city in Western Australia with a fiercely loyal local following.

name: North Melbourne
description: The North Melbourne Football Club, the Kangaroos, is known for its "Shinboner Spirit"—a legacy of grit, determination, and working-class resilience.
